claimText: The International Blue Flag Certification is awarded by the Foundation for Environmental Education (FEE) of Denmark.
evidenceExcerpt: Awarded by: Foundation for Environmental Education (FEE) of Denmark since 1985.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the organization responsible for the certification.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)

claimText: The Blue Flag Certification is an eco-label awarded to beaches, marinas, and tourism boats that meet 33 rigorous environmental, educational, and accessibility criteria.
evidenceExcerpt: A globally recognized eco-label awarded to beaches, marinas, and tourism boats. ... Certification requires meeting 33 rigorous criteria covering accessibility, education, environmental performance, and water quality.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the scope of the certification, specifically whether it applies only to beaches or includes marinas and boats.
distractorHooks: Awarded based solely on water quality criteria
Awarded to coastal cities rather than specific beaches or marinas

claimText: India currently has 18 Blue Flag beaches, with the highest number located in the state of Maharashtra.
evidenceExcerpt: Total beaches in India: 18, highest in Maharashtra.
examUse: Statement-based question on the distribution of Blue Flag beaches across Indian states.
distractorHooks: Highest number of Blue Flag beaches is in Goa
Highest number of Blue Flag beaches is in Kerala
Highest number of Blue Flag beaches is in Gujarat

claimText: Key Blue Flag beaches in India include Shivrajpur in Gujarat, Radhanagar in Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Eden in Puducherry, and Rushikonda in Andhra Pradesh.
evidenceExcerpt: List includes Shivrajpur Beach (Gujarat), Radhanagar Beach (Andaman & Nicobar), Eden Beach (Puducherry), and Rushikonda Beach (Andhra Pradesh).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching the Blue Flag beach to its respective State or Union Territory.
